name: autopoiesis-through-composition
title: Autopoiesis through composition
biological_story:
In a living cell, identity is not a given boundary but something the cell continuously MAKES:
metabolism produces the lipids that build the membrane, the membrane defines the volume that
concentrates the metabolism, and that volume feeds back into the reactions that remake the
membrane. Remove the production and the boundary decays — the identity is precarious, sustained
only by its own ongoing self-production. This investigation models that loop explicitly (study 1),
then asks whether the principle scales: whether the membrane can spatially contain its interior
against diffusion (study 2), whether sensing and moving toward food keep the precarious identity
alive (study 3), and whether it can grow, divide, and diversify into a population (study 4) — with
adversarial probes (study 5) checking the meter rejects systems that only LOOK self-producing.
The part the whole-cell-modeling lineage usually assumes — the membrane as a self-produced
boundary — is the part built here, from the molecular domain up.
question:
Can a precarious, self-bounding identity — a cell that produces its own boundary —
be assembled by composing existing parts, and how much of itself does each increment
produce? Progress is measured, not asserted: by operational closure (the autopoiesis
meter) and by precariousness (does the identity dissipate when self-production stops?).

executive.what_is_this:
A whole-cell model built up as a self-producing network, increment by increment.
The biological schema framework DRIVES acceptance: each study's verdict is the
autopoiesis meter (operational closure: gap = requires \ provides \ boundary) plus
the precariousness behavior test. The membrane — the self-produced boundary that
the whole-cell-modeling lineage models as a given — is the part we build.
executive.verdict:
A precarious, self-bounding identity is assembled by composition (study 1),
confined in space by its own membrane (study 2), kept alive by sensing and moving
toward food (study 3), and made to grow, divide, and diversify into a heterogeneous
population (study 4) — and the framework is now ADVERSARIALLY VALIDATED (study 5):
it REJECTS an externally-maintained mimic (persists when starved → not precarious)
and a network missing self-production (non-empty closure gap). The criteria
discriminate genuine self-production from superficial similarity; this is a test,
not just a demonstration.
executive.verdict_detail:
Every study now carries the rigor a skeptic asks for (8/8 on each; the
investigation scores 5/5): real, runnable NEGATIVE CONTROLS (externally-maintained
membrane via the pbg-superpowers Intervention; no-membrane containment; no-self-
maintenance reproduction), POSITIVE calibration controls, PARAMETER SWEEPS for the
deterministic studies and 12-SEED replication for the stochastic ones, tiered
findings (observation / mechanism / interpretation), excluded ALTERNATIVES, and
explicit FALSIFIABILITY. The 'agency / sense-making' reading (study 3) is marked an
interpretation of an engineered rule, and reproduction (study 4) is marked
engineered — both honestly bounded. The capstone adversarial study (5) closes the
loop: the same metric that accepts the self-producing loop rejects systems that
mimic it. Comparison to competing lenses (active inference, organizational /
constraint closure, viability theory) is recorded under competing_frameworks.
Open next steps remain (parasitic / random-cyclic probes) and are proposed in each
study's discovery_implications.

scientific_argument.main_claim: A minimal membrane/metabolism composite exhibits operational closure AND precariousness, and that self-producing identity extends compositionally — through spatial containment, adaptive chemotaxis, and growth/division — while surviving adversarial probes designed to break it.
evidence_for: Study 1: the loop closes (gap = ∅) and is precarious (collapses when starved); an externally-maintained-membrane negative control confirms precariousness tracks self-production, not external upkeep.

Studies 2–4: containment, chemotaxis, and growth/division each compose onto the loop and pass their literature-anchored bands; study-5 adversarial probes (external mimic, broken network) are correctly REJECTED.
Invariant regression: study-1's operational-closure + precariousness criteria remain PRESERVED when the later components are added.
evidence_against: Generality is largely single-axis (parameter / seed sweeps); alternate implementations and independent authoring are untested, so most claims are mechanism-level, not framework-level.
The membrane is modelled as a geometric boundary only (no transport / signalling / energetics), so operational closure is shown for a deliberately minimal ontology.

resolved_uncertainties: Operational closure is CLOSED modulo nutrient (gap = requires \ provides \ boundary = empty): the boundary and metabolism are self-produced.
The identity is precarious-but-maintained: volume collapses when self-production stops (fed vs starved).
remaining_uncertainties: Closure is shown at a single deterministic parameter point — robustness to supply_rate / decay is not yet demonstrated (a parameter sweep, not seeds, since the loop is an ODE).
The closure metric is not calibrated: no system that SHOULD fail closure has been run, so its discriminative power is unestablished (the reviewers' circularity concern).

alternate_hypotheses.hypothesis: Closure restates how the network was wired (bookkeeping), not an emergent organizational property.
alternate_hypotheses.why_plausible: membrane, metabolism and derived variables are explicitly coupled by construction.
discriminating_observables: closure_gap_size on an externally-maintained-membrane control (should NOT close)
closure_gap_size on a random-cyclic network (should NOT close)

followup_study_proposals.title: Closure robustness sweep
followup_study_proposals.motivation: Show closure holds across a supply_rate/decay range, not at one point (review C4 for deterministic models).

followup_study_proposals.title: Externally-maintained membrane (should FAIL)
followup_study_proposals.motivation: If the membrane is supplied externally rather than self-produced, precariousness and closure should FAIL — calibrating the metric and showing discriminative power (review C1/C2/C9).

findings.statement: The network is operationally closed: it self-produces 5/5 required types (lipid, membrane_lipids, nutrient, precursor, volume); only nutrient crosses the boundary. The cell produces its own boundary. Interface closure is CLOSED (the ports cover the requirements) and semantic closure is CLOSED (every self-produced type carries non-zero flux under the fed trajectory).
findings.next_action: Run an externally-maintained-membrane / random-cyclic negative control so closure has a system that SHOULD fail — calibrating the metric and establishing discriminative power rather than confirmatory fit (review C1/C2/C9).

findings.statement: The identity is precarious — a process, not a container. Fed, the self-produced boundary grows and is maintained (volume 2.97 → 9.37); starved of nutrient it dissipates (2.97 → 0.329). It persists only through continuous self-production.

studies.name: study-2-spatial-containment
studies.title: Study 2 — spatial containment: holding the individual together

resolved_uncertainties: The self-produced membrane counters diffusion and contains the metabolites; spatial precariousness holds.
remaining_uncertainties: A single deterministic PDE configuration — no parameter sweep over diffusion D / permeability k_perm.
The no-membrane comparison exists in code (membrane=False) but is not recorded as a declared negative control.

followup_study_proposals.title: Containment robustness sweep (D, k_perm)
followup_study_proposals.motivation: Show containment is robust across diffusion/permeability, not a single-configuration artifact (review C4).

followup_study_proposals.title: Record the no-membrane case as a negative control
followup_study_proposals.motivation: The membrane=False run should FAIL containment; declaring it makes the discriminative comparison explicit (review C2/C9).

findings.statement: The membrane holds the individual together against diffusion: the interior stays 60× more concentrated than the medium with the self-produced membrane, vs 4.4× with metabolism alone. The membrane — not metabolism — is what contains.
Containment is precarious: starved, the membrane decays, the boundary leaks, and the individual disperses — retaining only 4% of its containment. The individual is held together only as a process.

studies.name: study-3-adaptive-chemotaxis
studies.title: Study 3 — adaptive chemotaxis: move toward food to survive

resolved_uncertainties: Gradient sensing yields a robust survival advantage (2.33x +/- 0.26 over 12 seeds; 12/12 favour sensing).
The blind-motile control excludes 'mere movement': a motile agent that cannot sense survives far worse.
remaining_uncertainties: No positive/borderline calibration case (e.g. a weakly-sensing agent) to calibrate the survival metric across its range.
'Agency' and 'sense-making' remain an autopoietic INTERPRETATION of an engineered sensing->tumble rule, not demonstrated cognition.

alternate_hypotheses.hypothesis: The gain comes from spatial resource concentration, not directed sensing.
alternate_hypotheses.why_plausible: food is localised; even undirected agents starting near it could survive better.
discriminating_observables: survival under a uniform-resource control (no gradient) vs the localised gradient
survival of a weakly-sensing agent (borderline calibration case)

followup_study_proposals.title: Weakly-sensing agent (calibration case)
followup_study_proposals.motivation: A positive/borderline control to calibrate the survival metric across fail->borderline->pass (review C4).

followup_study_proposals.title: Parasitic / dependent agent
followup_study_proposals.motivation: An agent that exploits others' uptake blurs self-production vs dependence — stress-tests the framework (review C12).

findings.statement: Agency in service of survival: chemotactic agents climb the gradient, find food, and survive (79%), while blind agents random-walk and dissolve (only 35% survive) — a 2.3× survival advantage (mean ± 0.26 across 12 seeds; 12/12 favour sensing). Without sensing, the agent cannot maintain viability.
findings.next_action: Add a weakly-sensing (borderline) agent so the survival metric is calibrated across fail→borderline→pass, not just the pass/fail extremes (review C4).

findings.statement: Sense-making: the nutrient gradient is meaningful from the perspective of the precarious identity — chemotactic agents experience 1.39× more food than blind ones. Value (food = viable) is grounded in the cell's own viability, not assigned from outside. (Interpretation: 'sense-making' is the autopoietic reading of an engineered sensing→tumble response, not a claim of cognition.)

studies.name: study-4-growth-division
studies.title: Study 4 — growth & division: one individual becomes a heterogeneous population

resolved_uncertainties: One founder grows and divides into a heterogeneous population (robust across 12 seeds; reaches carrying every seed).
Division is precarious: daughters that inherit too little membrane dissolve.
remaining_uncertainties: Reproduction is ENGINEERED — explicit division, inheritance and partitioning rules — not shown to EMERGE from autopoietic self-maintenance (review C7).
No control distinguishing engineered copying from autopoietic self-reproduction.

alternate_hypotheses.hypothesis: The population growth demonstrates engineered copying, not autopoietic self-reproduction.
alternate_hypotheses.why_plausible: the split/inherit/partition rules are coded, not derived from the membrane/metabolism loop.
discriminating_observables: a no-self-maintenance reproduction control: copies that do not metabolise should still 'divide' but not persist
coupling of division rate to self-production rate

followup_study_proposals.title: Engineered vs autopoietic reproduction (negative control)
followup_study_proposals.motivation: Show the metric must couple reproduction to self-maintenance — copying without metabolism should not qualify (review C7).

findings.statement: The precarious identity is inherited: one founder grows and divides into a population of 300, and noisy partitioning + heritable mutation diversify the lineages (trait spread 0.11). One individual becomes a population of non-identical individuals.
Reproduction is precarious: 10% of daughters inherit too little membrane to maintain themselves and dissolve. Division has a viability cost — the same self-maintenance, now across generations.

studies.name: study-5-adversarial-probes
studies.title: Adversarial probes — can the framework be fooled?

resolved_uncertainties: The precariousness and closure criteria reject externally-maintained and structurally-incomplete systems — they have discriminative power, not just confirmatory fit.
remaining_uncertainties: Only two adversarial archetypes are probed; parasitic / partially-dependent and random-cyclic networks remain to be tested.

alternate_hypotheses.hypothesis: A cleverly-tuned random cyclic network might still score a closed gap.
alternate_hypotheses.why_plausible: closure is a structural bookkeeping check; a hand-crafted cycle could satisfy it without biological meaning.
discriminating_observables: operational_closure gap on a randomly-wired network
precariousness of a parasitic agent that exploits a host

followup_study_proposals.title: Parasitic / partially-dependent system
followup_study_proposals.motivation: Blur self-production vs dependence (parasites, endosymbionts, reduced genomes) and check where the criteria draw the line.

followup_study_proposals.title: Random cyclic network
followup_study_proposals.motivation: Test whether a meaningless cyclic network can be made to score a closed gap (would expose closure as mere bookkeeping).

findings.statement: The framework REJECTS an externally-maintained mimic: clamped from outside it persists when starved (precariousness fails), so the metric does not mistake external upkeep for self-production.

The framework REJECTS a network missing self-production: removing the membrane producer leaves a non-empty closure gap (['membrane_lipids']), so a structurally-incomplete network does not pass operational closure.
